transcript: [quiet] Yeah, we can— we can— We hear you loud and clear.
Alright! If you could turn you guys up, that’d be sweet!
Okay, we’re gonna try and… turn us up.

Yeah, so, uh… So we’ve—we’ve had a great turnout here, uh… LPC. It’s, uh… it’s the world premiere and we’re packed. Packed in. Packed in like sardines and everyone’s super, super thrilled. Uh… [unclear]—
Reaction of the—of the audience here. People have been—have been really please with the… with the film. Uh… I guess we can turn it over to—
—the audience or turn it over to you, LPC. It’s up to you. But, uh… does anybody have any questions for LPC? I can pass the mic around. He can’t hear you unless—
Yeah let’s hear it— Let’s hear it for— Let’s hear it for Dave and Vivek!—
District 39. Sergeant Peepers. How may I help you?
Yeah! Sergeant, I’ve got an opossum here. He took off with my wristwatch and my billfold. And, uh, this thing is runnin’ wil’! [delay]So I need— I need an officer out here. [exhales]
You’re calling the Indiana State Police.
I’m up here in Monroe County.
You need to call Monroe Sheriff’s Department then.
Well, we need to get a constable up here. This thing has my wristwatch, sir! [coughs] [delay]Can you, uh, send someone by—tase this animal?
[stammers] What’re you tryin’ to say up 'ere?! [exhales] [delay]
My billfold is in the teeth of this wild animal, sir! Well hello! [sniffs]Okay. Goodbye! Alright, everybody, let’s hear it for Dave and Vivek. Outstanding work. Thank you. Woo!
Hey, does— Does anybody have any, uh, questions for, uh, for LPC? Can I pass the mic around? Anybody here wanna… say something to him?
Sure! How’s everybody doin’? Let’s see what goin’ on… Anything at all!
If you were gonna pick your favorite American soap opera, what’s your favorite sport?
Soap opera… I loved All My Children. Uh… when I was younger. Um… seeing the Avalanche—The Colorado Avalanche—win the Stanley Cup the first time—their first year as a Colorado Franchise—
Patrick—wow! PATRICK—WOW! [delay]So that was very special. Yeah.
I’m sorry but the person you called has a voicemail box that has not been set up yet. Goodbye!
Okay, we have— We have somebody else here, uh… LPC.—
So I went ahead and, uh, commandeered that salt lick…
...and I was curious what you were thinkin’ about the de-beaking processes of the, uh, poultry farmers in, uh, this good nation.
[laughs] I feel that there’s far too much homogenization taking place.
Ya know, I really do. And, um… There—
Therefore, the granulated nature and makeup of the salt lick is…—
…greatly compromised! Greatly compromised! So, uh, that’s where I am with that and…
…if you can get one installed, uh… don’t lick it. Do not lick tha—! [delay] Or, lick it a lot! [delay]

—somethin’ up there. [mutters] Hwoooo-yahhhh! [delay, pitch-shifted]I hope that answers your question—homogenization, de-nuclearization. Along those lines. You can lick things. Go 'head and lick it! Woo! Woo! [delay]Yeah.
So anybody else wanna keep this conversation going?
Hey, thank you, um, so much for all the years of laughter!
I’ve always been curious about how you… come up with the names.
Like you pick the best names. I’m always curious how you’re—

Oh, thank you What’s the—? What’s the process behind that, you’re saying?Oh, ya knoow… things that stick in your… craw, things that stick in your memory. Usually— If I can remember 'em—I mean I don’t lose 'em—and I string 'em together at a later time.I’m not always as spontaneous as I once was or would like to be, so sometimes I write them down. Usually I make them up, but, uh… there’s a lotta ways that I come up—come up with those things. But yeah. Just thinking about it and writing 'em down and… saying 'em and saying 'em to myself. All sorts o' stuff goin' on up 'ere! [exhales]''I’m not gonna be trying 'em out on Alex Trebek anymore. So I hope you can appreciate that.—
We did not get your message, either because you are not speaking or because of a bad connection.
So, if you could have, uh, "jewels, wealth, or travel the world…?"
Are you still there? To disconnect press "1", to record your message press "2"—
Hi, this is [unclear]. So leave me a message and I’ll call ya back.
I like Jewel. I’m a fan of her music, I’m a fan of her… her work. [delay]
Sorry you are having trouble. Please try again later. Goodbye!

Jewel is an Alaskan treasure. I’d like to work with her one day. Like a drum cirlce-kinda thing. Like to have an animated Jewel special. I’m kinda lettin’ the cat outta the bag here a little bit but, uh… Yeah, I’m— We’re tryin’ to develop that right he— [delay, pitch-shifted]So if we can work that out I’m we wi—! [delay, pitch-shifted]I’m sure we will! [delay, pitch-shifted][laughs] So I hope that answers that question, ya know. [exhales]…or travel the world. It’s a toss-up.—
If you are satisfied with your message press "1". To listen to your message press "2". To erase and re-record press "3".—
Are you still there? You have reached the maximum time permitted for recording your message. If you are satisfied with your message press "1". To erase and re-record press "3".—
So— So, LPC, I’ve got a— I’ve got a question for you? What’s next?
Yeah, what’s— What’s next, LPC? What’s next? You— You always said you wanted to make the 30-year [unclear].—
Yeah! Uh… what next, you said?
Hi! You have reached Lutheran [unclear, perhaps "Smithsburg"]
Sorry, no one is available right now.

I… am working on Longmont Potion Castle 16. [coughs] [delay]I’m, uh… up to about 25, 30 minutes, so I’m doing that. And, ya know, it used to take me… three, four years to complete an album. And I will remind you, my good man, that these are, essentially, double, triple albums. Especially considering the length of the records that are coming out now.So… Yeah. Four, five years, it would take, often times. I hope seriously that it doesn’t take anywhere near that long. But I am working on that every chance I get. I want it to be really good.But otherwise, I just look forward to… talking to people about this movie. I mean, it’s so new, I wanna see how much enjoyment I can possibly get out of it.—

Oh, yeah! Yeahyeahyeah! I think I know where you’re goin’ with this, uh-huh, mhm!Well… start out with some talcum powder. Maybe like an emery board. Just take it slow! Get comfortable. Ya know, get real comfortable. Get horizontal. I’d like to see ya get horizontal, is what i’d like to see happen. So… make that happen—lemme know… how ya feel.
And then we will certainly take it from there! And I’m trying to get Mario Van Peebles on the line.
Maybe he can share his thoughts. Um… We shall see if he picks up…
Hey Mario, this is Tad. Hey, man, uh… Listen, I left a big, big duffel bag full o’ marshmallows, uh, over there on your… on your side—on your property. Can you hand 'em to me if I come over? [delay]
I’m not sure I understand what you’re saying.
I dropped— I dropped 'em on your side of the property. I just wanna get 'em back. Just got four or five-hundred in there. Does that work or…?
I’m not sur— I’m not sure— Who—who do you think you’re talking to?
To Mario! I got your number from the mailman. Ya feel me?—
And—and you—? You left—? You left some marshmallows on my property?
I did! I… uh, wasn’t my proudest moment, but yeah. I’d like to get 'em back.
I’m not— I’m not what you’re— Well what property is that? I—I think you might have the wrong Mario!
Well why don’t you… try listenin’ up, guy? I’m tryin’ to be polite here.
Your call has been forwarded [breaks up] automatic [breaks up] —age system. 4-4-3—[redacted]—
I could just take 'em back, ya know—just hop the fence and come get 'em, ya know what I’m sayin'!
Not— No, wha—? What—what proper—? What property are you talking about?
What’re you talkin’ about, tough guy?! You wanna arm wrestle or whadda you wanna do here?! Why don’t you just reimburse me?
Yeah. Marshmallows. Oh, sure! Big time! Oh, yeah! I go big! I don’t wait for Halloween.—
Ya know, I go now—any time I feel like it!—
Okay, I— I think you got the [laughing] wrong number! Okay. I think you have the wrong number. Bye.—
I think you need to reconsider, big man!

Whadda you say to that? Ohh! Goodbye to you then! Goodbye to you, sir!Mario Van Peebles, everybody! [pitch-shifted]Mario Van Peebles, everybody! [delay, pitch-shifted]How’ everybody doin’ up 'ere?! [delay, pitch-shifted, speed-shifted]

So I hope that answered your question…Ya know, it’s like:kan-kan-kan!di-ba-ba-boo![lolls tongue]wa-li-li-loo!Um… ya know, just anyth— uh… from your neighbor. I know about being neighborly and… it’s important to keep the peace. But ya know, if somebody pulls a Mario Van Peebles on ya, ya know, stand up for yourself! Stand up for your marshmallows! [delay, pitch-shifted, speed-shifted]Is all— ya know, it’s important to remember, so I can tell ya.Ohhh-wwaough! [delay, pitch-shifted, speed-shifted]I think we can all agree! So yeah.
What color is, uh… Tasmanian Syrup? And does it cure things?
Hello! You’ve reached Gavin Wallace Ellsworth. I am not available right now, so please leave your name and number after the beep and will get back to you as soon as I can.
Well, I was hoping to get a professional on the line and, uh…"Tasmanian Syrup"? That’s pretty weird. Pretty damn weird up 'ere! [exhales]Uh, there’s— There’s some healing properties, sure! Oh, yeah!
But I don’t know what color it is. What color is it? Jeez, that’s really weird! I heard fish respond really well to it.
So… I’m gonna try that out about 2 AM tomorrow!
"Cod"? Minnows... I’ve got about two to three-hundred minnows here. I suppose I can try here at home first. Uhm…—
Well… Put 'em in a bag and, uh… poke it!
[raps in early hip hop flow]I’m glad ya called but I’m not home!But I’ll be back before too long!Ya gotta leave ya name, ya gotta leave ya numba!Wait. For. The beep!
[exaggerated mimicry] ba-dah ba-dee gah-sus! I can do sus! [delay, pitch-shifted]I can get up 'ere! [pitch-shifted]—
At the tone please record your message.
We’re gonna get ready for the next screening but, uh…—
…thanks so much. Thanks so much for, uh…—
No, thank you! Oh my god, thank you…
…calling in. And we’ll— We’ll be— We’ll be, uh… we’ll— We’ll catch you at the next screening then!
Peace! Take care, everybody! Thank you so, so much! Thank you so much.
